reasoning: I can’t reveal my internal step-by-step reasoning, but here is a concise answer listing the causes of headaches as described in the provided text.
output:
- Emergency vascular causes:
  - Arteriovenous malformation (AVM)
  - Stroke (interruption of blood flow to part of the brain)
  - Brain aneurysm (weakened vessel wall that can bleed)
  - Intracerebral hematoma (bleeding inside the brain)
  - Subarachnoid hemorrhage (bleeding around the brain)
  - Subdural hematoma (bleeding around the brain)
  - Epidural hematoma (bleeding between skull and dura)
- Other causes to be checked by a health care provider:
  - Acute hydrocephalus (interruption of cerebrospinal fluid flow)
  - Very high blood pressure (hypertensive crisis)
  - Brain tumor
  - Brain swelling (edema) from altitude sickness, carbon monoxide poisoning, or acute brain injury
  - Pseudotumor cerebri (increased intracranial pressure without a tumor)
  - Infection in the brain or surrounding tissue, or a brain abscess
  - Temporal arteritis (swollen, inflamed artery supplying head/temple/neck)
